verbatim_text: How do we currently train models? We basically train on everything. We throw it all together in this big heap of data when we do pre-training. And we just train on all of the data that we have. So if models also learn like this, then that means that we've been training our models suboptimally. And so building up to this work, we kind of have two different hypotheses here. The first bit is that there exists some set of skills associated with your training data that an LLM that you're training will learn most efficiently from if it's arranged in a particular order. Now, the second hypothesis is that you can actually learn and exploit this ordering and improve your data efficiency. And so you can ultimately train an equivalently good model on less data or potentially train a better model on the same amount of data. Okay. So let's get concrete about what a skill actually means in this context. So when we have our data, we can partition it in a certain way where it's associated with some set of skills. And so some examples of this could be if your data is just the internet, you could have GitHub, all of the code on GitHub as one of the skills in your data set. And to a human, that would look like coding capabilities or something like that.
